what: Carceral, Policing, and Public-Health Data Retrieval and Analysis
title: Carceral, Policing, and Public-Health Data Retrieval and Analysis
text: Polite retrieval openly-published Canadian carceral police-oversight data.

title: Carceral, Policing, and Public-Health Data Retrieval and Analysis
text: v0.1 — development. focused interface carceral, policing, public-health open data — narrower upstream rmorie: retrieval, extraction, domain analysis API.

title: Carceral, Policing, and Public-Health Data Retrieval and Analysis
text: yet CRAN.

title: Carceral, Policing, and Public-Health Data Retrieval and Analysis
text: Function docs ship standard .Rd files. install, see ?rmorielite-package help(package = "rmorielite").

text: AGPL-3.0--later. upstream rmorie.

what: rmorielite: Polite Retrieval of Canadian Carceral and Police-Oversight Data — rmorielite-package
title: rmorielite: Polite Retrieval of Canadian Carceral and Police-Oversight Data — rmorielite-package
text: focused interface upstream rmorie toolkit retrieving, mining, analysing open carceral, policing, police-oversight, public-health data – correctional tracking segregation (OTIS), special-investigations oversight bodies (SIU, ARSAU), municipal police services (Toronto, Chicago, New York, Vancouver), substance-use surveys (Canadian Cannabis Survey, CPADS, related Health Infobase / CIHI tables).

title: rmorielite: Polite Retrieval of Canadian Carceral and Police-Oversight Data — rmorielite-package
text: Polite default. Inter-request interval enforced via small -disk state file; default floor one request every 0.25 seconds. See rmorielite_fetch(). Cache default. Downloads land R session temporary directory (tempdir()); set RMORIELITE_PERSISTENT_CACHE=1 opt persistent cache via tools::R_user_dir(). Second fetches URL served disk. See rmorielite_cache_dir(). Provenance default. SHA256 verification available alongside every fetched file. Focused re-export. rmorielite imports upstream rmorie toolkit rmoriebricklayer, re-exporting domain provenance APIs, adds small httr2-based polite, cache-aware fetch layer.

title: rmorielite: Polite Retrieval of Canadian Carceral and Police-Oversight Data — rmorielite-package
text: rmorielite focused interface carceral, policing, public-health data domain. upstream multi-domain rmorie package available https://github.com/rootcoder007/rmorie.

title: rmorielite: Polite Retrieval of Canadian Carceral and Police-Oversight Data — rmorielite-package
text: Maintainer: Vansh Singh Ruhela vsruhela@proton.(ORCID) Authors: Vansh Singh Ruhela vsruhela@proton.(ORCID)

what: Resolve the package cache directory — rmorielite_cache_dir
title: Resolve the package cache directory — rmorielite_cache_dir
text: Returns directory rmorielite uses cached resources. default sub-tree R session temporary directory (tempdir()) – files cleaned automatically R exits, line CRAN policy packages must write outside session temp area without explicit user consent.

title: Resolve the package cache directory — rmorielite_cache_dir
code: rmorielite_cache_dir(   which = c("cache", "data", "config"),   create = TRUE,   persistent = NULL )

title: Resolve the package cache directory — rmorielite_cache_dir
text: One "data", "cache", "config"; matches argument tools::R_user_dir(). Defaults "cache". create Logical. TRUE (default), create directory recursively yet exist. persistent Logical NULL. NULL (default), reads environment variable RMORIELITE_PERSISTENT_CACHE. TRUE, uses tools::R_user_dir(); FALSE, uses tempdir().

title: Resolve the package cache directory — rmorielite_cache_dir
text: character scalar: absolute path directory.

title: Resolve the package cache directory — rmorielite_cache_dir
text: opt persistent cache survives R sessions, set environment variable RMORIELITE_PERSISTENT_CACHE=1 pass persistent = TRUE. persistent location follows R's official convention via tools::R_user_dir() – macOS ~/Library/Application Support/.../rmorielite, Windows %LOCALAPPDATA%\R\.../rmorielite, Linux $XDG_DATA_HOME/R/rmorielite (~/.local/share/R/rmorielite XDG_DATA_HOME unset).

title: Resolve the package cache directory — rmorielite_cache_dir
code: rmorielite_cache_dir()                  # default: session tempdir #> [1] "/tmp/RtmpvQVZFJ/rmorielite/cache" rmorielite_cache_dir(persistent = TRUE, create = FALSE) # opt in to R_user_dir #> [1] "/home/runner/.cache/R/rmorielite"

what: Resolve a CKAN resource by name pattern — rmorielite_ckan_resolve
title: Resolve a CKAN resource by name pattern — rmorielite_ckan_resolve
text: Queries CKAN open-data portal's package_show endpoint returns metadata first resource whose name field matches supplied regular expression. CKAN powers many government open-data portals (Ontario, UK, Canada, US federal); resource UUIDs occasionally change portal updates resource names usually stable, name-pattern matching survives portal reslugs.

title: Resolve a CKAN resource by name pattern — rmorielite_ckan_resolve
Resolve a CKAN resource by name pattern — rmorielite_ckan_resolve
text: portal Base URL CKAN portal, without trailing slash. Example: "https://data.ontario.ca". package_slug CKAN package slug. Example: "data--inmates--ontario". name_pattern PCRE-compatible regex applied case-insensitively resource's name field. first match wins. timeout Request timeout seconds. Default 30.

title: Resolve a CKAN resource by name pattern — rmorielite_ckan_resolve
text: list elements name, url, format, resource_id, package_slug, NULL package found, API call fails, resource matches.

title: Resolve a CKAN resource by name pattern — rmorielite_ckan_resolve
code: # \donttest{ # Live network call; wrapped in try() so the example never errors if the # portal is unreachable under R CMD check --run-donttest. try({   res <- rmorielite_ckan_resolve(     portal        = "https://data.ontario.ca",     package_slug  = "data-on-inmates-in-ontario",     name_pattern  = "Restrictive.?Confinement.*Detailed.?Dataset"   )   if (!is.null(res)) res$url }) #> [1] "https://data.ontario.ca/dataset/09f7fc65-d3bb-4ca8-8b84-1cdc3ef73c36/resource/5a0c5804-a055-4031-9743-73f556e43bb4/download/a01_restrictive_confinement_detailed_dataset.csv" # }

title: Polite, cache-aware HTTPS fetch — rmorielite_fetch
text: Downloads single URL per-user cache directory (see rmorielite_cache_dir()) using httr2 built-throttling exponential-backoff retry 429/5xx. non-empty cached copy target path already exists, download skipped cached path returned.

title: Polite, cache-aware HTTPS fetch — rmorielite_fetch
code: rmorielite_fetch(   url,   dest_basename = NULL,   rate = 4,   force = FALSE,   max_tries = 3L )

title: Polite, cache-aware HTTPS fetch — rmorielite_fetch
text: url URL fetch. dest_basename Filename (path) cache result. Defaults URL's basename. rate Requests per second. Default 4. force TRUE, re-download even cached copy exists. Default FALSE. max_tries Maximum retry attempts transient errors (429, 5xx). Default 3.

title: Polite, cache-aware HTTPS fetch — rmorielite_fetch
text: character scalar: absolute path cached file.

title: Polite, cache-aware HTTPS fetch — rmorielite_fetch
text: Throttling retry delegated httr2::req_throttle() httr2::req_retry(). default rate cap 4 requests per second matches polite--default conventions used major public-data CKAN portals.

title: Polite, cache-aware HTTPS fetch — rmorielite_fetch
code: # \donttest{ # Live network call; wrapped in try() so the example never errors if the # portal is unreachable under R CMD check --run-donttest. try({   path <- rmorielite_fetch(     "https://data.ontario.ca/api/3/action/package_list"   )   file.exists(path) }) #> [1] TRUE # }

title: rmorielite 0.1.0
text: Initial release. rmorielite_cache_dir() — per-user cache/data/config directories. Defaults sub-tree R session tempdir() (CRAN-policy compliant). Persistent storage tools::R_user_dir() opt-via persistent = TRUE RMORIELITE_PERSISTENT_CACHE env var. rmorielite_ckan_resolve() — resolves CKAN resource package slug + name-pattern match. Survives portal reslugs resource UUIDs assumed stable. Uses httr2. rmorielite_fetch() — polite, cache-aware HTTPS fetch using httr2 token-bucket throttling (4 req/s default) exponential- backoff retry 429/5xx.
